title: Yacht Charter in Dubrovnik: 2026 Costs, Boats & Local Tips
html:
For a yacht charter in Dubrovnik, depart from the Old Town harbour or ACI Marina Dubrovnik and expect to pay from €1,200 to €8,000 per week for a crewed motor yacht, with boats available to book through BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Dubrovnik?

A crewed motor yacht in Dubrovnik costs from €1,200 to €8,000 per week in 2026. Prices vary by boat size, season, and duration. A half-day small motorboat hire starts around €350, while a full-day catamaran for a group of 12 can cost €1,800. For bareboat sailing yachts (no crew), expect €1,500 to €4,000 per week. For the latest deals, compare verified boat hire Dubrovnik options on BoatHire24. You can boat hire Dubrovnik for your chosen dates.

Where to set off in Dubrovnik

Most charters begin from ACI Marina Dubrovnik, a modern facility just west of the Old Town. The nearby port of Gruž handles larger yachts and offers easy provisioning. For day trips, set off from the Old Town harbour (Port of Dubrovnik) for quick access to the Elaphiti Islands. Further afield, the medieval town of Cavtat, 15 km south, has a well-sheltered marina. The bay at Lopud, one of the Elaphiti Islands, is a popular anchorage for lunch stops. For overnight trips, head to the island of Korčula, a two-hour sail away, with its historic harbour and vineyards.

Which boat to rent: a guide to the best types

The best boat for your Dubrovnik charter depends on group size and desired luxury level. One option worth a look is the Princess F70 listing.

  1. Motor yacht, Ideal for fast cruising between islands, sleeps 8-12 guests, from €3,000 per week. For a premium example, see the Princess F70 listing on BoatHire24, which offers a certified crew and flybridge for sunbathing.
  2. Sailing yacht, Perfect for a traditional, quiet experience, sleeps 6-10 guests, from €1,500 per week.
  3. Catamaran, Best for stability and social space, sleeps 8-12 guests, from €2,000 per week.
  4. RIB or speedboat, Great for day trips and water sports, sleeps up to 6 guests, from €350 half-day.

How much does a typical week cost in 2026?

Weekly costs range from €1,200 for a shared tour to €8,000 for a high-end crewed yacht.

  • Shared tour (half-day): €70, €120 per person.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, self-drive): €350, €600 total.
  • Catamaran (full-day, crewed): €1,800, €3,500 total for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed motor yacht (weekly): €3,000, €8,000 total for 8-10 guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Dubrovnik?

In Croatia, a valid boating licence is required for bareboat charters, but crewed charters do not need one. For self-drive rentals, most operators require an ICC or equivalent, and the minimum age is typically 25. Always confirm the specific licence requirements with your boat provider. For skippered charters, the captain holds the licence, so you need only a passport. The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking.

Tip: Anchor in the Zupa Bay near Cavtat for a calm swimming spot with a beach bar, best visited in early morning to avoid summer crowds.

At a glance

  • Price band: €350, €8,000 per week.
  • Peak months: July and August.
  • Trip length: half-day to one week.
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests.
  • Currency: EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Dubrovnik

Can I charter a yacht for just one day?

Yes, many operators offer day charters from Dubrovnik, including trips to the Elaphiti Islands lasting 8 hours.

What is the best time to charter a yacht in Dubrovnik?

The best time is May to September, with July and August being the hottest and busiest months; June and September offer pleasant weather and fewer crowds.

Are meals and drinks included in the crewed charter price?

Most crewed charters include a skipper, fuel, and basic insurance, but meals and drinks are usually extra unless specified as all-inclusive.

How far are the nearby islands from Dubrovnik?

The Elaphiti Islands are only 15 minutes to 90 minutes away by boat, while the island of Korčula is about 50 nautical miles south-west.

Why Dubrovnik is a premier destination for boat hire in 2026

With its sheltered marinas, clear Adriatic waters, and network of historic islands, Dubrovnik offers exceptional yacht charter opportunities. Whether you choose a crewed motor yacht from ACI Marina or a bareboat catamaran from Gruž, booking through BoatHire24 gives you access to verified boats and local expertise. Boat owners can also list their yacht on BoatHire24 to reach travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dubrovnik and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
